text: The envelope sat on the counter for three hours before they opened it, and they made a cup of tea first, and then another, and reorganized a shelf, and then finally picked it up with both hands
She checked the rearview mirror eleven times on the drive to the interview and adjusted her collar at every red light and arrived twenty minutes early and sat in the parking lot watching the clock
He rehearsed the sentence in his head so many times that by the time he knocked on the office door he had forgotten how it was supposed to start and said something else entirely

The phone buzzed with a notification from the doctor's portal and they set the phone face down and went for a walk around the block and then another block and then came home and the phone was still there
She tried on three different outfits and then the first one again and then a fourth one and then the first one a third time and was now running late and still standing in front of the mirror

The presentation was loaded on the laptop and the laptop was plugged in and the backup was on a flash drive and the flash drive was in the left pocket and he checked the pocket four times during the elevator ride
They wrote the text message and deleted it and wrote it differently and deleted that and wrote the original version again and then set the phone on the table and stared at it for a while

The pilot's voice on the intercom said mild turbulence and she tightened the seatbelt that was already tight and placed both hands flat on the armrests and counted the rivets on the wing outside the window
He arrived at the restaurant first and chose the seat facing the door and straightened the silverware and moved the salt shaker to the left side and then moved it back
The student opened the exam booklet and read the first question and read it again and picked up the pen and set it down and read the question a third time and then began writing very slowly
She stood in the wings listening to the emcee read her introduction and her mouth was dry and she took a sip of water and then worried that she had taken too much and would need the restroom onstage
They tested the microphone three times at sound check and then once more when no one was looking and tapped it gently with their finger just to confirm it was still working
The swimmer stood on the block and shook out her arms and adjusted her goggles and shook out her arms again and the referee had not even called the heat yet
He packed the suitcase the night before and then unpacked it in the morning and repacked it in a different order and added a second pair of shoes and then removed them

The proposal was in the jacket pocket and he kept touching the outside of the pocket to make sure the box was still there, which it was, every time, because objects do not leave jacket pockets on their own

She read the recipe all the way through twice before starting and then measured the flour three times, leveling it with a knife, because the dinner guests included his parents and this was the first time
The new employee walked into the open-plan office on the first morning and could not remember where they had been told to sit and stood near the entrance holding a coffee for too long
He printed five copies of his resume even though the interviewer would only need one, and he put them in a folder, and the folder in a bag, and the bag was now on his lap on the subway

The dentist's waiting room had a fish tank and she counted the fish and then counted them again and then noticed a magazine from last year and read an article about kitchen renovations without absorbing a single word
They loaded the moving truck and drove four hours to the new city and parked on the unfamiliar street and sat in the cab looking at the building that was now supposed to be home
She saved the document and then saved it again and then emailed it to herself and then saved it to the cloud and then opened the cloud version to make sure it had saved correctly
The silence after the question lasted five seconds and then ten and during those ten seconds he considered and rejected eleven different answers, none of which he would remember afterward
They double-checked the stove knobs before leaving and then came back from the car to check them again and stood in the kitchen looking at the knobs which were clearly in the off position

The child held the parent's hand on the way to the school doors and at the threshold squeezed harder and asked one more question about where the lunchroom was even though they had gone over it five times
She opened the banking app and the balance was lower than expected and she closed the app and opened it again as though the number might have changed in the three seconds between
The actor waited in the hallway outside the audition room and ran lines silently, lips moving, and every time the door opened for someone else a small jolt traveled from their stomach to their throat
He parked the car at the trailhead and looked at the map and then at the sky and then at the map again and decided to bring both the rain jacket and the fleece even though the forecast said clear

The couple sat in the adoption agency waiting room and the wife arranged and rearranged the contents of the folder on her lap and the husband's right foot tapped a rhythm on the floor that matched nothing
She entered the pool for the first lap and the water was the same temperature it always was but today it felt colder and her stroke felt wrong and the clock on the wall seemed faster than usual
They stood at the podium and looked out at the audience and adjusted the microphone that did not need adjusting and took a drink of water and adjusted the microphone again
He googled the symptoms for the fourth time that day and each search added a new term and each new term led to a different page and none of the pages agreed and he closed the laptop and opened it

The passport was in the carry-on and she checked three times at the gate and once more on the jetway and considered checking again but the flight attendant was greeting her and she had to keep walking
The dinner party guests were arriving in forty minutes and the sauce had broken and the internet offered six different ways to fix it and he tried the first one with shaking hands and a whisk
She practiced the handshake with the doorknob, adjusting the firmness, because the last interviewer had mentioned something about first impressions and she had been thinking about it for three days

They were next in line at the karaoke machine and the song they had chosen suddenly seemed like the wrong one and they considered switching but the screen was already loading and the room was turning to look
The lab results would be posted by five o'clock and it was three-fifteen and they cleaned the entire apartment in the interim, including behind the refrigerator, which they had never cleaned before
He held the fishing rod over the ice hole and his hands were steady but his jaw was clenched because the ice had made a sound twenty minutes ago that he could not identify and had not repeated
The bride stood behind the double doors listening to the music change and her bouquet shook slightly and she gripped it tighter and the wedding planner said breathe and she realized she had not been

They reread the lease agreement for the third time, slowly, moving a finger under each line, because the rent was most of their paycheck and the deposit was nonrefundable and the pen was in their hand

She stepped onto the stage for the spelling bee and the microphone was taller than she was and the auditorium was larger than her school and she spelled her own name wrong in her head before they gave her the first word
